title: sEEG Passive listening to natural speech
description: This stereoelectroencephalography (sEEG) dataset comprises intracranial recordings from neurosurgical patients passively listening to natural conversational speech. Participants heard 30-45 second passages of speech and answered comprehension questions, with data collected across 6 blocks of 7 passages each. The dataset includes depth electrode recordings from Ad-Tech Spencer Probes and, for two patients, grid and strip electrode arrays, providing high-resolution neural activity during speech perception.
methods_description: Intracranial recordings were collected from June 2018 to August 2019 using depth electrodes (Ad-Tech Spencer Probes with 10 leads spaced 3-7mm apart) and, for patients SD012 and SD022, grid and strip electrodes. Scalp reference and ground electrodes were 13mm, 2.5M single lead subdermal electrodes (Rochester Electro-Medical, serial S81025-A-24RM). Participants passively listened to 30-45 second passages of conversational speech followed by 2-alternative forced choice comprehension questions across 6 blocks.
